title: PhD
biography: Mahmoud Ghannoum is an Egyptian-American microbiologist who coined the term 'mycobiome' and is a leading researcher on fungal infections and biofilms.
aiSummary: Mahmoud Ghannoum is a microbiology specialist at Case Western Reserve University. Based in Cleveland.

affiliations.role: Professor and Director of the Center for Medical Mycology

Development of Interpretive Breakpoints for Antifungal Susceptibility Testing: Conceptual Framework and Analysis of In Vitro-In Vivo Correlation Data for Fluconazole, Itraconazole, and Candida Infections

A large-scale North American study of fungal isolates from nails: The frequency of onychomycosis, fungal distribution, and antifungal susceptibility patterns
citations.journal: Journal of the American Academy of Dermatology

timeline.title: Coined the term 'mycobiome'
timeline.description: Named and pioneered the study of the fungal component of the human microbiome
